rules.N.1.3: VAERS: The Batch Sender Identifier can be the DUNS ID (9 Digit Identifier and DUNS OID 1.3.6.1.4.1.519.1) or other Identifier.
rules.N.1.4:
Post-Market: For ICSR sent to the postmaket route use the value "ZZFDA".
Pre-Market: For ICSR sent to the premaket route use the value "ZZFDA_PREMKT".
VAERS: FDA requires that these identifiers correspond to the FDA ESG connection (e.g., WebTrader or AS2 B2B) used to send the ICSR submission to VAERS.  

WebTrader Accounts: CBER VAERS
AS/2 Accounts: CBER_VAERS
rules.N.2.r.2:
Post-Market: Senders must recieve FDA approval of their Message Sender Identifier before beginning FAERS Submissions.  The Message Sender Identifier can be the DUNS ID (9 Digit Identifier and DUNS OID 1.3.6.1.4.1.519.1) or other Identifier with FDA approval.
Pre-Market: Senders must recieve FDA approval of their Message Sender Identifier before beginning FAERS Submissions.  The Message Sender Identifier can be the DUNS ID (9 Digit Identifier and DUNS OID 1.3.6.1.4.1.519.1) or other Identifier with FDA approval.
VAERS: Senders must recieve FDA approval of their Message Sender Identifier before beginning FAERS Submissions.  The Message Sender Identifier can be the DUNS ID (9 Digit Identifier and DUNS OID 1.3.6.1.4.1.519.1) or other Identifier with FDA approval.  ICSR batches with non-approved sender identifiers will be rejected.
rules.N.2.r.3:
Post-Market: FDA requires that these identifiers correspond to the FDA ESG connection (e.g., WebTrader or AS2 B2B) used to send the ICSR submission to FAERS. 

Use the value "CDER". If Batch Receiver Identifier (N.1.4) = "ZZFDA", then Message Receiver Identifier (N.2.r.3) must be 'CDER'
Pre-Market: FDA requires that these identifiers correspond to the FDA ESG connection (e.g., WebTrader or AS2 B2B) used to send the ICSR submission to FAERS. 

For CDER IND use the value "CDER_IND" or
For CBER IND use the value "CBER_IND" or
For CDER IND-exempt BA/BE use the value  "CDER_IND_EXEMPT_BA_BE"

If Batch Receiver Identifier (N.1.4) = "ZZFDA_PREMKT", then Message Receiver Identifier (N.2.r.3) must be either 'CDER_IND' or 'CBER_IND' or 'CDER_IND_EXEMPT_BA_BE'
VAERS: FDA requires that these identifiers correspond to the FDA ESG connection (e.g., WebTrader or AS2 B2B) used to send the ICSR submission to VAERS.  

WebTrader Accounts: CBER VAERS
AS/2 Accounts: CBER_VAERS
rules.N.2.r.4: VAERS: FDA requires full date/time stamp and UTC offset (i.e. ‘CCYYMMDDhhmmss[+/-ZZzz]’).  When no half-hour increment use "00" in the last two positions: EX: <creationTime value="20141217083947-0800/>
rules.C.1.1:
Post-Market: Guidance: Use the same Sender’s (case) Safety Report Unique Identifier (C.1.1) for all previously submitted reports.

Required for FDA to review, process and archive (21 CFR 312.32)
Pre-Market: Guidance: Use the same Sender’s (case) Safety Report Unique Identifier (C.1.1) for all previously submitted reports.

Required for FDA to review, process and archive (21 CFR 312.32)
VAERS: Note: The Specifications for Preparing and Submitting Postmarket Individual Case Safety Reports (ICSRs) for Vaccines document provides additional user guidance for this field.
rules.C.1.3: Pre-Market: If Batch Receiver Identifier (N.1.4) = ZZFDA_PREMKT then Type of Report (C.1.3) must be 2=Report from Study
rules.C.1.6.1.r.2:
Post-Market: Compression is not used for US reporting and encoding is limited to B64.
Pre-Market: Compression is not used for US reporting and encoding is limited to B64.
VAERS: Compression is not used for US reporting and encoding is limited to B64.
rules.C.1.7:
Post-Market: FDA does not support use of the nullFlavor 'NI' for this data element in initial submissions. Initial submissions with nullFlavor NI will be rejected.
Pre-Market: FDA does not support use of the nullFlavor 'NI' for this data element in initial submissions. Initial submissions with nullFlavor NI will be rejected.
VAERS: Note: FDA has applied a regional rule requiring an observation code to specify values for the different types of "true" or "false".  See FDA element "Local Criteria Report Type" for more information.
rules.FDA.C.1.7.1:
Post-Market: Use element values 4 for remedial action to prevent an unreasonable risk of substantial harm to the public health.

Use element values 5 for malfunction with no associated adverse event.

If Combination Product Report Indicator (FDA.C.1.12) = true and Does This Case Fulfil the Local Criteria for an Expedited Report? (C.1.7) = true, Observation Code: (Value allowed: 1, 4)

If Combination Product Report Indicator (FDA.C.1.12) = true and Does This Case Fulfil the Local Criteria for an Expedited Report? (C.1.7) = False or NI, Observation Code: (Value allowed: 2, 5)

If Combination Product Report Indicator (FDA.C.1.12) = False or NI and Does This Case Fulfil the Local Criteria for an Expedited Report? (C.1.7) = true, Observation Code: (Value allowed: 1) 

If Combination Product Report Indicator (FDA.C.1.12) = False or NI and Does This Case Fulfil the Local Criteria for an Expedited Report? (C.1.7) = False or NI, Observation Code: (Value allowed: 2)
Pre-Market: For 15-Day or 7-Day Expedited report, if Does This Case Fulfil the Local Criteria for an Expedited Report? (C.1.7) = True and Type of Report (C.1.3) = 2 (Report from Study) then Observation Code: (Value allowed: 1, 6) respectively.
VAERS: If Combination Product Report Flag = true
and C.1.7 = true, Observation Code:  (Value options: 5, 15)

If Combination Product Report Flag = true
and C.1.7 = False or NI, Observation Code: (Value options: Non Expedited AE, Malfunction only/No AE)

If Combination Product Report Flag = False or NI: 
and C.1.7 = true, Observation Code set to 1 (15 day) 

If Combination Product Report Flag = False or NI: 
and C.1.7 = False or NI, Observation Code set to 3 (Non Expedited AE)
rules.C.1.8.1: VAERS: Note: The Specifications for Preparing and Submitting Postmarket Individual Case Safety Reports (ICSRs) for Vaccines document provides additional user guidance for this field.
rules.C.1.9.1.r.1:
Post-Market: If Other Case Identifiers in Previous Transmissions (C.1.9.1) = true then Source(s) of the Case Identifier (C.1.9.1.r.1) cannot be empty
VAERS: Note: FDA will provide a warning (and not reject) if C.1.9.1 = true and C.1.9.1.r.1 is not provided.
rules.C.1.9.1.r.2:
Post-Market: If Other Case Identifiers in Previous Transmissions (C.1.9.1) = true and Source(s) of the Case Identifier (C.1.9.1.r.1) is available then Case Identifier(s) (C.1.9.1.r.2) cannot be empty
VAERS: Note: FDA will provide a warning (and not reject) if C.1.9.1 = true and C.1.9.1.r.1 is not provided.
rules.C.1.10.r: Pre-Market: If Patient (name or initials) (D.1) = AGGREGRATE, then Identification Number of the Report Which Is Linked to This Report (C.1.10.r ) should be provided  for all Sender’s (case) Safety Report Unique Identifier (C.1.1) that makes up an Aggregate Analysis as per 312.32(c)(1)(i)(C)
rules.C.1.11.1: Post-Market: Initial report cannot be a nullified or amendent report
rules.FDA.C.1.12:
Post-Market: Guidance: Required per FDA Regional guidance.
VAERS: Required per FDA Regional guidance.
rules.C.2.r.1.2:
VAERS: This field is required when C.2.r.5 = 1
Note: MSK is not allowed for US cases when C.2.r.3 Reporter's Country Code = "US"
rules.C.2.r.1.4:
VAERS: This field is required when C.2.r.5 = 1
Note: MSK is not allowed for US cases when C.2.r.3 Reporter's Country Code = "US"
rules.C.2.r.2.3:
VAERS: This field is required when C.2.r.5 = 1
Note: MSK is not allowed for US cases when C.2.r.3 Reporter's Country Code = "US"
rules.C.2.r.2.4:
VAERS: This field is required when C.2.r.5 = 1
Note: MSK is not allowed for US cases when C.2.r.3 Reporter's Country Code = "US"
rules.C.2.r.2.5:
VAERS: This field is required when C.2.r.5 = 1
Note: MSK is not allowed for US cases when C.2.r.3 Reporter's Country Code = "US"
rules.C.2.r.2.6:
VAERS: This field is required when C.2.r.5 = 1
Note: MSK is not allowed for US cases when C.2.r.3 Reporter's Country Code = "US"
rules.C.2.r.2.7:
VAERS: This field is required when C.2.r.5 = 1
Note: MSK is not allowed for US cases when C.2.r.3 Reporter's Country Code = "US"
 
When submitting a nullflavor response also include the telecom prefix within the value attribute to correctly reference the related telecom type, as shown in examples:
<telecom value=“tel:1112223333”/>
<telecom nullflavor=“NASK” value=“tel”/>

Note: Nullflavor should have value of "tel" only and without ":"
rules.FDA.C.2.r.2.8:
Post-Market: When submitting a nullflavor response also include the telecom prefix within the value attribute to correctly reference the related telecom type, as shown in examples: 
<telecom value=“mailto:email@address.com”/>
<telecom nullflavor=“NASK” value=“mailto”/>
 
Note: Nullflavor should have value of "mailto" only and without ":"
Pre-Market: When submitting a nullflavor response also include the telecom prefix within the value attribute to correctly reference the related telecom type, as shown in examples: 
<telecom value=“mailto:email@address.com”/>
<telecom nullflavor=“NASK” value=“mailto”/>
 
Note: Nullflavor should have value of "mailto" only and without ":"
VAERS: Regionally required for all reports.  MSK is not allowed for US cases for the primary reporter for the regulatory purposes.  
 
When submitting a nullflavor response also include the telecom prefix within the value attribute to correctly reference the related telecom type, as shown in examples: 
<telecom value=“mailto:email@address.com”/>
<telecom nullflavor=“NASK” value=“mailto”/>
 
 Note: Nullflavor should have value of "mailto" only and without ":"
rules.C.2.r.3:
VAERS: This field is required when C.2.r.5 = 1
Note: MSK is not allowed for US cases when C.2.r.3 Reporter's Country Code = "US"

rules.C.4.r.2:
VAERS: The location of the attachment file name must follow the <text mediaType> attribute based upon the following example:  text mediaType="text/plain" representation="B64">
        <reference value="SUMMARY OF CLINICAL HISTORY.txt"/>. If the file extension in the filename does not match the media type, the ICSR file will be rejected.
rules.C.5.2:
Pre-Market: If Type of Report (C.1.3) is 2=Report from study and Message Receiver Identifier (N.2.r.3) = 'CDER_IND' or 'CBER_IND' then Study Name (C.5.2) should be in the format Study ID$Abbreviated Trial Name

The  Study ID should be the same value used in the Study Tagging file format of the eCTD submission.
rules.C.5.4:
Pre-Market: If Patient (name or initials) (D.1) = AGGREGRATE, then Observation Code: (Value allowed: 1) for Study Type Where Reaction(s) / Event(s) Were Observed (C.5.4)

If Type of Report (C.1.3) is 2=Report from study and Message Receiver Identifier (N.2.r.3) = 'CDER_IND' or 'CBER_IND' then Observation Code: (Value allowed: 1) for Study Type Where Reaction(s) / Event(s) Were Observed (C.5.4)
rules.FDA.C.5.5a:
Pre-Market: If Type of Report (C.1.3) is 2=Report from study and Message Receiver Identifier (N.2.r.3) = 'CDER_IND' or 'CBER_IND' then IND Number where AE Occurred (FDA.C.5.5a) is required. The format must be "123456" for IND report without any prefix.  

For IND safety reports submitted from an aggregate analysis (312.32(c)(1)(i)(C)) from trials conducted under more than one IND, use the “Parent” IND number
rules.FDA.C.5.5b: Pre-Market: If Type of Report (C.1.3) is 2=Report from study and Message Receiver Identifier (N.2.r.3) = 'CDER_IND_EXEMPT_BA_BE' then Pre-ANDA Number where AE Occurred (FDA.C.5.5b) is required. The format must be "234567" for IND-exempt BA/BE study without any prefix.
rules.FDA.C.5.6.r: Pre-Market: If IND Number where AE Occurred (FDA.C.5.5a) is populated, then IND number of cross reported IND (FDA.C.5.6.r) is required. Use nullFlavor=NA if there are no other cross reported IND
rules.D.1:
Post-Market: The element must use nullFlavor "NA" where no patient is involved. (e.g. Medication error, Compounding)

For combination product, if a single report is reported for a malfunction without an adverse event, the element value must use nullFlavor "NA".

For combination product, if there are multiple malfunction reports with no adverse event, then the element value should be “SUMMARY”.
Pre-Market: For Aggregate Report, the element value must be “AGGREGATE”. 

If Element Value for Type of Report (C.1.3) is 2 (=Report from study) and IND Number where AE Occurred (FDA.C.5.5a) is provided and 
Identification Number of the Report Which Is Linked to This Report (C.1.10.r ) is populated then Patient (name or initials) (D.1) must have the value  'AGGREGATE'
VAERS: When this section is included, use the expanded FDA Regional data elements (Patient Name Prefix, Patient First Name, Patient Middle Name, Patient Last Name) in this field
rules.D.2.1:
Pre-Market: If Patient Initial (D.1) is "AGGREGRATE" then Date of Birth (D.2.1) is Optional
VAERS: FDA requires that at least one of the data elements in the Age Information section be populated (D.2.1, FDA.D.2.1, D.2.2, D.2.2.1), preferably Date of Birth (D.2.1) and/or Age at the Time of Vaccination (FDA.D.2.1)
If Local Criteria Report Type = Malfunction Only (No AE) , then this field is optional.
rules.D.2.2a:
VAERS: Note: FDA requires that at least one of the data elements in the ICH Age Information section be populated (D.2.1, Age at time of Vaccination, D.2.2, D.2.2.1), preferably Date of Birth (D.2.1) and Age at the Time of Vaccination. Regional format supports use of decimals as follows: 3 digits before the decimal and two digits after the decimal
If Local Criteria Report Type = Malfunction Only (No AE) , then this field is optional.
rules.D.2.2b: VAERS: This field is required if D.2.2a is provided.
rules.D.2.2.1a:
VAERS: Note: FDA requires that at least one of the data elements in the ICH Age Information section be populated (D.2.1, Age at time of Vaccination, D.2.2, D.2.2.1), preferably Date of Birth (D.2.1) and Age at the Time of Vaccination. Regional format supports use of decimals as follows: 3 digits before the decimal and two digits after the decimal
If Local Criteria Report Type = Malfunction Only (No AE) , then this field is optional.
rules.D.2.2.1b: VAERS: This field is required if D.2.2.1.a is provided.
rules.D.2.3:
VAERS: This field is required when the previous age fields (D.2.1, Age at time of Vaccination, D.2.2, D.2.2.1) were omitted.
If Local Criteria Report Type = Malfunction Only (No AE) , then this field is optional.
rules.D.7.1.r.1b: VAERS: US will verify MedDRA code format and if not correct, ICSR file will be rejected
rules.D.8.r.2a: VAERS: This field is required when D.8.r.2b is populated.
rules.D.8.r.2b: VAERS: For U.S. source reports: If provided, the value must be a two- or three-segment NDC code separated by hyphen(s).
rules.D.8.r.3a: VAERS: Optional data element. FDA is currently not using this field
rules.D.8.r.3b: VAERS: Optional data element. FDA is currently not using this field
rules.D.8.r.7b: VAERS: US will verify MedDRA code format and if not correct, ICSR file will be rejected
rules.D.9.1: Pre-Market: If Results in Death (E.i.3.2a) is ture then Date of Death is required.
rules.D.10.8.r.2a: VAERS: This field is required when D.10.8.r.2b is populated.
rules.D.10.8.r.2b: VAERS: For U.S. source reports: If provided, the value must be a two- or three-segment NDC code separated by hyphen(s).
rules.D.10.8.r.3a: VAERS: Optional data element. FDA is currently not using this field
rules.D.10.8.r.3b: VAERS: Optional data element. FDA is currently not using this field
rules.FDA.D.11.r.1:
Post-Market: Appropriate nullFlavor must be used if FDA.D.11.r (Patient Race Code) is not reportable.

If Patient (name or initials) (D.1) is nullFlavor "NA" or "SUMMARY" then use nullFlavor: NA
Pre-Market: Appropriate nullFlavor must be used if FDA.D.11.r (Patient Race Code) is not reportable.

If Patient (name or initials) (D.1) is "AGGREGATE"  then use nullFlavor: NA
VAERS: If Local Criteria Report Type = Malfunction Only (No AE) , then this field is optional. Otherwise, required for all cases.  The data element is supported using an additional <observation> and the regional terminology code C17049 .
rules.FDA.D.12:
Post-Market: Appropriate nullFlavor must be used if FDA.D.12 (Patient Ethnicity Code) is not reportable.

If Patient (name or initials) (D.1) is nullFlavor "NA" or "SUMMARY" then use nullFlavor: NA
Pre-Market: Appropriate nullFlavor must be used if FDA.D.12 (Patient Ethnicity Code) is not reportable.

If Patient (name or initials) (D.1) is "AGGREGATE"  then use nullFlavor: NA
VAERS: If Local Criteria Report Type = Malfunction Only (No AE) , then this field is optional. Otherwise, required for all cases.  The data element is supported using an additional <observation> and the regional terminology code C16564.  No more than one (1) ethnicity code can be used.
rules.FDA.E.i.3.2h:
Post-Market: This is an outcome for device reporting and medication errors.
Pre-Market: For Pre-Market safety reports the value of the data element must always be ‘NI’'-
rules.E.i.4: VAERS: At least one of the data elements for E.i.4, E.i.5 or Ei.6 must be populated.
rules.E.i.5: VAERS: At least one of the data elements for E.i.4, E.i.5 or Ei.6 must be populated.
rules.E.i.6a: VAERS: At least one of the data elements for E.i.4, E.i.5 or Ei.6 must be populated.
rules.G.k.1:
Post-Market: At least one product must be reported with Observation Code: (Value allowed: 1, 3, 4)

The first product (under section G) should have this data element answered as ‘1’ (=Suspect) or ‘3’ (=Interacting), unless the product has at least once device constituent part where Malfunction (FDA.G.k.12.r.1) is “true”, in which case the Observation Code: (Value allowed: 1, 3, 4)
Pre-Market: Characterization of Drug Role (G.k.1) is required to identify Suspect and Concomitant products: 

For IND ICSRs, Only use Observation Code: (Value allowed: 1, 2, 3)

For IND-exempt BA/BE ICSRs, Use Observation Code: (Value allowed: 1, 2, 3, 4)
VAERS: The first product (under section G) must have this data element answered as '1’ (=Suspect) or ‘3’ (=Interacting).
rules.FDA.G.k.1.a: Post-Market: For Similar Device Observation Code: (Value allowed: 1) is only allowed if Combination Product Report Indicator (FDA.C.1.12) is 'true', Malfunction (FDA.G.k.12.r.1) is 'true' and Characterisation of Drug Role (G.k.1) = '4' (Drug Not Administered) under 21 CFR 4.102(c) and 803.50 using OID 2.16.840.1.113883.3.989.5.1.2.1.1.8
rules.G.k.2.1.1a:
VAERS: FDA requires at least one of: MPID (G.k.2.1.1), Substance TermID (G.k.2.3.r.2b), or Substance Name (G.k.2.3.r.1)

This field is required when G.k.2.1.1b is populated.
rules.G.k.2.1.1b: VAERS: For U.S. Source Reports: If provided, the value must be a two- or three-segment NDC code separated by hyphen(s).
rules.G.k.2.1.2a: VAERS: Optional data element. FDA is currently not using this field
rules.G.k.2.1.2b: VAERS: Optional data element. FDA is currently not using this field
rules.G.k.2.3.r.1: VAERS: FDA requires at least one of: MPID (G.k.2.1.1), Substance TermID (G.k.2.3.r.2b), or Substance Name (G.k.2.3.r.1)
rules.G.k.2.3.r.2a: VAERS: This field is required if G.k.2.3.r.2b is populated
rules.G.k.2.3.r.2b:
VAERS: FDA requires at least one of: MPID (G.k.2.1.1), Substance TermID (G.k.2.3.r.2b), or Substance Name (G.k.2.3.r.1)

This field is required if G.k.2.3.r.2a is populated
rules.G.k.2.3.r.3a: VAERS: This field is required if G.k.2.3.r.3b is populated
rules.G.k.3.1:
VAERS: (1)  For U.S. source reports: If Application Number is provided for the first product (under section G), then it must be a BLA number.

(2) For U.S. source reports: If provided, this data element must follow FDA format: prefixID, where prefix equals "BLA", "NDA", or "ANDA", and ID is a code of 6-25 alphanumeric characters with no intervening space between prefix and ID. If the prefix = "BLA" then the ID portion must be a 6 digit number (e.g., "BLA123456"). If the prefix = "NDA" or "ANDA", the ID portion must be of 6-25 alphanumeric characters.
 
(3)  For U.S. source reports: If no BLA is provided, a warning will be generated.
rules.G.k.4.r.9.2a: VAERS: This field is required when G.k.4.r.9.2b is provided.
rules.G.k.4.r.9.2b:
Post-Market: EDQM or FDA SPL Dosage Form
Pre-Market: FDA SPL Dosage Form codelist
VAERS: FDA SPL Dosage Form codelist
rules.G.k.4.r.10.2a: VAERS: This field is required when G.k.r.10.2.b is provided.
rules.G.k.4.r.10.2b:
Post-Market: EDQM or FDA SPL Dosage Form
Pre-Market: FDA SPL Dosage Form codelist
VAERS: FDA SPL or E2B codelist
rules.G.k.4.r.11.2a: VAERS: This field is required when G.k.r.11.2.b is provided.
rules.G.k.4.r.11.2b:
Post-Market: EDQM or FDA SPL Dosage Form
Pre-Market: FDA SPL Dosage Form codelist
VAERS: FDA SPL or E2B codelist
rules.G.k.9.i.1: VAERS: Optional data element; however, when used, Reaction Identifier (FDA.E.i.1.3) is required when providing causality assessment for reactions captured in Section E and drugs in Section G
rules.G.k.9.i.2.r.1:
Pre-Market: Default Source of Assessment (G.k.9.i.2.r.1 ) to "Sponsor" and Include Investigator Assessment in H.1

Required, if Element Value for Type of Report (C.1.3) is 2 (=Report from study) and IND Number where AE Occurred (FDA.C.5.5a) is provided
rules.G.k.9.i.2.r.2:
Pre-Market: Default Method of Assessment (G.k.9.i.2.r.2) to “FDA” to differentiate from other assessment methods.

Required, if Element Value for Type of Report (C.1.3) is 2 (=Report from study) and IND Number where AE Occurred (FDA.C.5.5a) is provided
rules.G.k.9.i.2.r.3:
Pre-Market: For IND Safety Reports, at least one suspect product should have relatedness of drug to reaction/event

For Result of Assessment (G.k.9.i.2.r.3) use the value ""Suspected"" or ""Not Suspected"""

Required, if Element Value for Type of Report (C.1.3) is 2 (=Report from study) and IND Number where AE Occurred (FDA.C.5.5a) is provided
rules.FDA.G.k.10a: Pre-Market: The rule is applicable only for IND-Exempt BA/BE study. If Pre-ANDA Number where AE Occurred (FDA.C.5.5b) is present, then the Observation Code: (Value allowed: 1, 2) must be used to describe the drug’s role in the IND-Exempt BA/BE study. Use nullFlavor 'NA' for all other drugs or if information is not available.
rules.FDA.G.k.10.1: VAERS: Optional data element; however, if used FDA values must be valid SPL codes.  Data element is needed to determine whether the product is a combination product or compounded product
rules.FDA.G.k.12.r.1:
Post-Market: If Local Criteria Report Type (FDA.C.1.7.1) = 5, then Malfunction (FDA.G.k.12.r.1) must be 'true' for at least one suspect product
VAERS: This field must be set to true for at least one suspect product per ICSR, when C.1.7.1 Local Criteria Report Type = Malfunction Only (No AE)
rules.FDA.G.k.12.r.2.r:
Post-Market: Classification of the type of follow-up ICSR. Use the same manfacturer control number (to ensure the report is not misidentified in FAERS as an initial report).
VAERS: One or more codes can be provided.
rules.FDA.G.k.12.r.3.r:
Post-Market: Required, if Malfunction (FDA.G.k.12.r.1) is 'true'. FDA will only validate format. One or more codes can be provided.
Provide valid FDA or IMDRF device problem code
VAERS: This field is required when Malfunction = true. FDA will only validate format. One or more codes can be provided.
rules.FDA.G.k.12.r.4:
Post-Market: If Combination Product Report Indicator (FDA.C.1.12) is true, then provide the device brand name and the common device name. Either can be null however, if both are null a value for Device Product Code (FDA.G.k.12.r.6) is required
VAERS: At least one of the 3 must be reported with a non null value: <brandname> or <commondevicename> or <productcode> for the device constituent part when Malfunction = true

There will always be two <name> elements present – the first being the brand name, and the second being the common device name.  Either can be null however, if both are null a value for FDA.G.k.12.r.6 is required
rules.FDA.G.k.12.r.5:
Post-Market: If Combination Product Report Indicator (FDA.C.1.12) is true, then provide the device brand name and the common device name. Either can be null however, if both are null a value for Device Product Code (FDA.G.k.12.r.6) is required
VAERS: At least one of the 3 must be reported with a non null value: <brandname> or <commondevicename> or <productcode> for the device constituent part when Malfunction = true

There will always be two <name> elements present – the first being the brand name, and the second being the common device name.  Either can be null however, if both are null a value for FDA.G.k.12.r.6 is required
rules.FDA.G.k.12.r.6:
Post-Market: If both Device Brand Name (FDA.G.k.12.r.4) and Common Device Name (FDA.G.k.12.r.5) are null, then the value for Device Product Code (FDA.G.k.12.r.6) is required
VAERS: At least one of the 3 must be reported with a non null value: <brandname> or <commondevicename> or <productcode> for the device constituent part when Malfunction = true

Use the NCI C code that is corresponding to the product code.
rules.FDA.G.k.12.r.9: Post-Market: UDI PI, the lot or batch number within which a device was manufactured
rules.FDA.G.k.12.r.10: Post-Market: Use '1' or '2' to describe the operator,  If None Applicable, Then Specify value '3'.
rules.FDA.G.k.12.r.11.r:
Post-Market: Required, if Malfunction (FDA.G.k.12.r.1) is 'true' and Local Criteria Report Type (FDA.C.1.7.1) = 4 (5-Day). One or more codes can be provided.
VAERS: This field is required when Malfunction = true , C.1.7 = Yes and Local Criteria for Report = 5 day. One or more codes can be provided.
